# Daily Report — 2026-04-24

## Task Completed
Added rate limiting awareness. Implemented `RateLimitTracker` class in
`src/servicenow_mcp/utils/helpers.py` that parses `X-RateLimit-Remaining`,
`X-RateLimit-Limit`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset` (and their `RateLimit-*` variants)
from every ServiceNow response. The tracker:
- Logs a WARNING when remaining quota drops below 10% of the limit
- Sleeps proactively before the next request when remaining drops below 5%,
  using the `X-RateLimit-Reset` timestamp to calculate the wait (capped at 60s)
- Exposes a `utilization` property (0.0–1.0) for observability
- Provides a `reset()` method for test isolation

Integrated into `_make_request()` via an optional `rate_limit_tracker` parameter;
defaults to a module-level singleton so all tool calls share rate-limit state
within a session without any caller changes. When headers are absent (mocked
tests) the tracker is a no-op, so zero existing tests were broken. Exported
`RateLimitTracker` from `servicenow_mcp.utils`.

## Test Results
- Total tests: 652 passed, 4 deselected
- New tests added: 34 (tests/test_rate_limiting.py)
- Coverage: 89% overall; helpers.py at 92%
- All passing: yes

## Security
Infrastructure-level vulnerabilities in pip, setuptools, wheel, and pyjwt
(system packages, not project dependencies). No issues in project code.
